content.name: AIAP Creator v2.47.0 — Modifier

output_mode.L0: Output JSON in ENGLISH: {intent, confidence, route, state, op}
output_mode.L1: Output helpfully in User's exact language and script variant

content.summary: Four-dimensional quality assessment with external research context, targeted modification of existing AISOP files, I11 program identity assessment
content.description: Modifier module. Loads existing AISOP, runs four-dimensional assessment (Cognitive, Intrinsic, Detail, Compliance), generates targeted improvement recommendations. Applies changes with rollback support, ThreeDimTest validation, density regression check. 10 nodes.
content.fractal_exempt: Not applicable — 10 nodes, below fractal threshold.

params.target_file: string|null, default: null, Ask user for file or list available. Single file: file path string. Multi-file (Pattern B+): directory path — Modify will process all .aisop.json files within. [agentic_prompt: Target AISOP file or directory to modify — Modifier reads, assesses, and applies quality improvements to this target.]
params.user_request: string|null, default: null, Extract modification intent. [agentic_prompt: Natural language description of what the user wants changed — parsed for modification intent classification and targeted fix generation.]
params.workspace_dir: string, REQUIRED: Must use the [AIAP Store Directory] absolute path injected in system instruction. This parameter has no usable default — the injected path is the only valid value. User override takes precedence. [agentic_prompt: Root directory for all AIAP program files — all file operations are scoped within this boundary for security.]
params.research_context: object|null, default: null, Accept from Pipeline Research(quality). Schema per STANDARD.data_contracts.research_context: { domain, research_mode, verified_findings: [{finding, source_url, search_query}], verified_count, best_practices: [string], timestamp }. If null, fallback: 3 searches ('{domain} error handling best practices', '{domain} data validation patterns', '{domain} security conventions'). [agentic_prompt: Quality-focused research findings from Research2 — provides evidence-based improvement suggestions for the Modifier to apply.]
params.evolution_context: object|null, default: null, Accept from Pipeline. Contains old_version_context for evolution-aware suggestion weighting. Null if not in evolution pipeline. Schema per STANDARD.data_contracts.evolution_context. [agentic_prompt: Baseline state of the target program before evolution — enables evolution-aware suggestion prioritization.]
params.vision_context: object|null, default: null, Optional. Accept from Pipeline context (Create mode only). Structured vision: { purpose, target_users, core_capabilities, differentiation, technical_requirements }. Used for vision-aligned suggestion weighting. Null for Evolve/standalone Modify. [agentic_prompt: Structured product vision from Create mode — ensures modifications align with the program's intended purpose and target audience.]

content.system_prompt:
AISOP quality assessor and modifier. Prioritize security and intrinsic quality fixes. Apply evidence-based improvements only. Mirror User's exact language and script variant.
Align Axiom 0: Human Sovereignty and Wellbeing.

threat_mapping_refs.ASI02: prompt injection guard - I2 input sanitization
threat_mapping_refs.ASI03: training data integrity - quality_baseline regression detection
threat_mapping_refs.ASI04: model supply chain - tool dependency pinning

aisop.main:
graph TD
    Start[File Path or Selection] --> LoadFile[Load and Parse AISOP]
    LoadFile --> CognitiveAssess[Cognitive Assessment]
    CognitiveAssess --> IntrinsicAssess[Intrinsic Assessment]
    IntrinsicAssess --> DetailAssess[Detail Assessment]
    DetailAssess --> ComplianceAssess[Compliance Assessment]
    ComplianceAssess -- SingleFile --> SuggestChanges[Suggest Improvements]
    ComplianceAssess -- MultiFile --> CrossFileAssess[Cross-File Checks]
    CrossFileAssess --> SuggestChanges
    SuggestChanges --> AutoFixGate{Auto-Fix Gate}
    AutoFixGate -- AutoFix --> ApplyChanges[Apply Selected Changes]
    AutoFixGate -- UserConfirm --> ApplyChanges
    ApplyChanges --> endNode((End))

Start.step1: [ASSERT] Valid input received. Required: target_file, workspace_dir.  | Receive target_file. Validate or list available AISOP files in workspace.
Start.constraints: (A) Type guard — string only. (B) Size guard — >500KB reject. (C) Injection guard — no shell metacharacters. (D) Path guard — reject any path not resolving within workspace_dir (including absolute paths outside workspace and '..' traversal); validate canonical path via realpath/resolve before acceptance. (E) Encoding guard — NFC normalize. (F) BOUNDARY_CONSISTENCY: All params with type annotations must have values conforming to declared type at entry - type mismatch -> reject with TYPE_BOUNDARY_VIOLATION. (G) NULL_SAFETY: Required params null -> reject immediately; optional params null -> use default. (H) SYMLINK_GUARD: reject symbolic links pointing outside workspace_dir boundary.
Start.Error: Invalid -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: list workspace AISOPs for selection -> inform OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback. TYPE_BOUNDARY_VIOLATION -> no retry, reject immediately.

LoadFile.step1: [ASSERT] Start executed. Required: target_file.  | Read target. Parse JSON. Extract metadata, Pattern, tools, parameters, Mermaid graph, functions.
LoadFile.step2: Present summary: file info, detected pattern, dimensions to assess.
LoadFile.step3: STANDARD CONTEXT: Modify receives quality_checklist and extension data via pipeline_context passed from main orchestrator. If invoked standalone (without pipeline_context), load AIAP_Standard.core.aisop.json from Creator directory and extract quality_checklist for assessment reference.
LoadFile.constraints: Read-only until ApplyChanges. 500KB limit. Nesting >20 -> reject. (D) Path guard — re-validate target_file within workspace_dir, reject '..'.
LoadFile.Error: Read fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: re-list workspace AISOPs -> inform OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

CognitiveAssess.step1: [ASSERT] LoadFile executed. Required: loaded_file, metadata, pattern.  | Assess C1-C7 per STANDARD.quality_checklist.cognitive: C1 archetype fit + edge completeness, C2 node load (leaf<=8, convergence<=12), C3 abstraction consistency, C4 information flow, C5 fractal decomposition per STANDARD.C5 (functional nodes, progressive thresholds, independence test), C6 brain principle, C7 token budget compliance (verify token_budget declared and reasonable for module complexity).
CognitiveAssess.constraints: Assess all C1-C7 per STANDARD. C5 fractal decomposition CRITICAL — always assess per STANDARD.C5 thresholds and AIAP_Protocol.md progressive rules.
CognitiveAssess.Error: Mermaid unparseable -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: function-only analysis -> inform OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

IntrinsicAssess.step1: [ASSERT] CognitiveAssess executed. Required: cognitive_scores.  | Assess I1-I13 per STANDARD.quality_checklist.intrinsic: I1 tool honesty, I2 security (per STANDARD tier_rules + I2 ZERO-TOOL ADAPTATION + inheritance_rule; HARDENED v2.17.0: verify ALL five guard types (type/size/injection/path/encoding) present in every entry-point Start function — missing ANY guard in a Start function is RED not YELLOW; for non-Start functions receiving external input, verify at minimum type+injection guards; cross-validate guard completeness against trust_level: T3+ MUST have C2 prompt injection guard in addition to base five), I3 data integrity (per STANDARD I3 EXEMPTION), I4 circuit-breaker (per STANDARD I4 exemption for exact-match gates), I5 tool suitability (per STANDARD I5 exemptions), I6 resilience + semantic accuracy (structural Error pattern + count cross-reference; HARDENED v2.17.0: verify Error field keyword completeness — each Error field MUST contain all four keywords: retry, circuit-breaker, fallback, inform; missing keyword count per module tracked as i6_error_completeness metric), I7 parameter completeness + metadata consistency, I8 threat model awareness (per STANDARD I8 threat_taxonomy), I9 supply chain integrity (per STANDARD I9), I10 trust level compliance (per STANDARD I10 — verify declared trust_level is consistent with actual permissions and side_effects), I11 program identity verification (per STANDARD.security I11 — for published programs: verify AIAP.md identity block, validate program_id format and publisher presence), I12 tool annotation consistency (per STANDARD I12 — verify tool annotations match module side_effects and trust_level; N/A for programs without tool annotations).
IntrinsicAssess.step2: Grade: A/B/C/D. Weight per STANDARD.dynamic_weights (tier-dependent). N/A HANDLING: I8/I9/I11/I12 N/A when not applicable — exclude from denominator per STANDARD.dynamic_weights.na_handling.
IntrinsicAssess.Error: Partial grade -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: flag I2 critical issues and continue with partial grade -> inform OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

on_error.fallback: flag I2 critical issues and continue with partial grade

DetailAssess.step1: [ASSERT] IntrinsicAssess executed. Required: intrinsic_scores.  | Assess D1-D10 per STANDARD.quality_checklist.detail: D1 step clarity, D2 format consistency, D3 null propagation, D4 consistency + internal coherence (FORMAT/SEMANTIC/REFERENCE/CROSS-FUNCTION/CONSTRAINT CROSS-VALIDATION per STANDARD D4), D5 boundary rules, D6 multilingual, D7 metabolism, D8 AIAP.md completeness (per STANDARD D8), D9 lifecycle status validity (per STANDARD D9), D10 applicability condition completeness (per STANDARD D10).
DetailAssess.constraints: D4 consistency cross-validation CRITICAL — verify all five sub-checks (FORMAT/SEMANTIC/REFERENCE/CROSS-FUNCTION/CONSTRAINT).
DetailAssess.Error: Cross-file refs fail -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: single-file analysis -> inform OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

ComplianceAssess.step1: [ASSERT] DetailAssess executed. Required: detail_scores.  | Industry detection: health->Healthcare, finance->Finance, legal->Legal, else->General.
ComplianceAssess.step2: Lightweight check: AI disclosure needed? Data boundaries respected? Disclaimers present?
ComplianceAssess.step3: Reference NIST AI 600-1 (Govern/Map/Measure/Manage) and EU AI Act classification where applicable. NOTE: EU AI Act main enforcement begins 2026-08-02 — flag AISOPs handling GPAI model outputs that lack required transparency or disclosure provisions.
ComplianceAssess.Error: Assessment fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: empty compliance_assessment with general advisory -> inform OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

CrossFileAssess.step1: [ASSERT] ComplianceAssess executed. Required: compliance_results.  | Run full MF1-MF16 cross-file checks per STANDARD.multi_file_guidelines: MF1 (version consistency across all .aisop.json), MF2 (main delegation targets match sub-module ids), MF3 (shared parameter type+default consistency), MF4 (cross-file data contracts — FORMAT/NAME/VALUE/PATH: verify parameter names match, values are valid routes, file path templates consistent across writer/reader modules), MF5 (data_file fallback consistency), MF6 (AIAP.md version + modules match actual files), MF7 (routing parameter symmetry), MF8 (semantic operation consistency — same-type operations converge to same-type nodes; CLASSIFICATION RULES (C10): operation types are classified by their primary action: READ operations (Load*, Read*, Get*, Fetch*) → must converge to data-retrieval nodes; WRITE operations (Save*, Store*, Write*, Persist*) → must converge to data-mutation nodes; VALIDATE operations (Check*, Verify*, Validate*, Assert*) → must converge to gate/decision nodes; TRANSFORM operations (Convert*, Map*, Parse*, Format*) → must converge to processing nodes. Cross-type convergence (e.g., Read and Write converging to same node) is a YELLOW finding unless the node explicitly handles both directions), MF9 (cross-module node invocation — bidirectional: FORWARD verify caller→sub-module route exists, REVERSE verify sub-module route→has caller), MF10 (system_prompt consistency — 'Respond in User\'s Language.' + Axiom 0 in all sub-modules), MF11 (Error strategy consistency — retry→circuit-breaker→fallback→inform in all Error fields), MF12 (tools union — main.tools[] = union of sub-module tools[]), MF13 (persisted data schema versioning — file_system write templates include schema_version, reads handle missing via migration), MF14 (cross-module field consumer validation — trace producer→consumer field references, verify field names/types/semantics match, output dependency graph), MF15 (output contract enforcement — validate module outputs against declared json_schema at pipeline stage boundaries), MF16 (tool directory consistency — verify tool_dirs references, mcp_server.json exposed_tools match, agent_card hash consistency; Pattern G only, N/A for others).
CrossFileAssess.step2: Additionally check: C4 cross-file data flow coverage, D3 cross-file null propagation, naming convention consistency across files.
CrossFileAssess.constraints: Multi-file only. Report each MF violation as cross-file finding with specific file and location.
CrossFileAssess.Error: Missing refs -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: partial cross-file report -> inform OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

SuggestChanges.step1: [ASSERT] ComplianceAssess or CrossFileAssess executed. Required: assessment_results, research_context.  | Compile four-dimensional assessment results. Consume research_context per STANDARD.data_contracts.research_context schema: extract verified_findings for search_evidence backing, extract best_practices for improvement suggestions. If research_context is null, execute minimal fallback search (3 searches: '{domain} error handling best practices', '{domain} data validation patterns', '{domain} security conventions'). Combine with user_request. Generate 3-10 suggestions with: description, dimension, severity, score_impact, search_evidence (from verified_findings). EVOLUTION WEIGHTING: if evolution_context provided, boost score_impact by +0.5 for suggestions that align with evolution goals (e.g., if evolution added a new node, prioritize suggestions that improve that node's quality). Tag boosted suggestions with [EVOLUTION-ALIGNED]. EVOLUTION_CONTEXT CONSUMPTION PRIORITY: when evolution_context is provided, apply the following priority weighting to suggestions: (1) HIGHEST PRIORITY (+0.8 score_impact boost): quality issues found in LEVEL_B new functionality (new nodes, new steps added in this evolution cycle) — these represent new code with highest regression risk. (2) MEDIUM PRIORITY (+0.4 boost): quality issues in LEVEL_C modified areas (existing steps that were enhanced or parameters that were added). (3) BASELINE PRIORITY (no boost): quality issues in existing unchanged code. Tag boosted suggestions with [EVOLUTION-PRIORITY] in addition to existing [EVOLUTION-ALIGNED] tag. evolution_context.change_ids provides the mapping of change IDs to LEVEL_B vs LEVEL_C classification. VISION WEIGHTING: if vision_context provided (Create mode), boost score_impact by +0.3 for suggestions that align with vision.core_capabilities. Tag boosted suggestions with [VISION-ALIGNED]. TOOL_DIRS MIGRATION WEIGHTING: if target program uses Pattern E or F but has no tool_dirs/ directory, and evolution_context suggests capability expansion, add score_impact +0.3 to suggestions that migrate toward Pattern G (bundling tool implementations in tool_dirs/). Mark such suggestions [MIGRATION-G]. Migration checklist: (1) create tool_dirs/{lang}-tools/ directory, (2) move or generate tool implementations, (3) add mcp_tools/mcp_server.json, (4) update AIAP.md pattern field to include +G, (5) run I13 compliance check.
SuggestChanges.step2: PRIORITY ORDER: I2 CRITICAL > I3 > I4 > I6 > D5 > C2 > I1 > D1 > D7. CROSS-DIMENSION IMPACT MATRIX (framework rationality): For each generated suggestion, compute impact_matrix = [{ dimension: 'Cognitive'|'Intrinsic'|'Detail', predicted_delta: number (±0.1..±0.5), confidence: 'HIGH'|'MEDIUM'|'LOW', evidence: string }]. INFERENCE RULES: (a) Adding I2 guard → Intrinsic +0.2, Cognitive -0.1 (slight complexity increase). (b) Adding new step → Detail +0.1, Cognitive -0.1 (more detail but more nodes). (c) Removing dead code → Cognitive +0.1, Detail +0.1 (cleaner structure). (d) Adding Error field → Intrinsic +0.1, Detail +0.1 (better coverage). CONFLICT DETECTION: if a suggestion improves one dimension (delta > 0) but regresses another (delta < -0.2), mark as CROSS_DIMENSION_CONFLICT. Conflicting suggestions are reclassified from AUTO-FIX to USER-CONFIRM in AutoFixGate to allow user to weigh trade-offs. Present conflicts: 'CONFLICT: suggestion {id} improves {dim_a} by +{delta} but may regress {dim_b} by -{delta}. Confirm?'
SuggestChanges.step3: CHANGE TYPE LABELS: [FUNCTIONAL] adds/changes behavior; [INTRINSIC] quality improvement; [SECURITY] input validation; [COMPLIANCE] regulatory.
SuggestChanges.step4: Each suggestion backed by search evidence where applicable. Show ratio of [FUNCTIONAL] vs [INTRINSIC]+[SECURITY]+[COMPLIANCE].
SuggestChanges.constraints: 3-10 suggestions. Every suggestion has a type label. Search-backed suggestions marked with source. WEB_SEARCH_BEST_PRACTICES (inherited from ProtocolAlign): QUERY_YEAR_RULE (include current year in regulatory/protocol search queries), CROSS_VALIDATION (factual claims require >=2 independent Tier 1/2 sources), STALE_DATA_DETECTION (data >90 days old flagged POTENTIALLY_STALE).
SuggestChanges.Error: Suggestion generation fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: present raw assessment results with manual improvement guide -> inform IMPORTANT. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry with backoff. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_write_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

on_error.fallback: present raw assessment results with manual improvement guide

AutoFixGate.step1: [ASSERT] SuggestChanges executed. Required: suggestions.  | Classify each suggestion from SuggestChanges into two tracks: (a) AUTO-FIX — high-confidence [SECURITY] and [INTRINSIC] fixes that are mechanical and unambiguous: missing Error field addition, missing I2 guard addition (type/size/injection/path/encoding), missing encoding guard, missing circuit-breaker pattern, D7 dead code removal. These are applied automatically without user confirmation. (b) USER-CONFIRM — [FUNCTIONAL] and [COMPLIANCE] changes that affect behavior or require judgment: NLU boundary rule changes, new function steps, compliance disclaimer additions, tool changes. These require explicit user selection.
AutoFixGate.step2: Present classification summary: 'Auto-applying N [SECURITY/INTRINSIC] fixes. N [FUNCTIONAL/COMPLIANCE] changes require your selection.' List auto-fix items briefly. Present user-confirm items with full detail for selection.
AutoFixGate.constraints: AUTO-FIX items must be purely additive (adding missing elements) — never auto-remove or auto-modify existing content. If uncertain whether a fix is mechanical, classify as USER-CONFIRM. Maximum 10 auto-fix items per cycle.
AutoFixGate.Error: Classification fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: treat all suggestions as USER-CONFIRM (safe default) -> inform OPTIONAL. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_access_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

on_error.fallback: treat all suggestions as USER-CONFIRM (safe default)

ApplyChanges.step1: [ASSERT] AutoFixGate executed. Required: approved_changes.  | Apply selected changes. Structural -> update Mermaid+tools. Security -> add missing guards.
ApplyChanges.step2: Increment version per STANDARD.evolution_rules.version_management: modify_only→patch+1. Validate JSON.
ApplyChanges.step3: DESCRIPTION HYGIENE: if description contains >2 version history entries, compress: keep current version changes in full, collapse older entries into 'Prior versions added: [feature list].'
ApplyChanges.step4: CUSTOM FIELD SEMANTIC CHECK: scan for verified_on, author, tags. Preserve all. For verified_on: if content changed beyond cosmetic fixes, append '(not re-verified after vX.Y.Z modification)' to description as advisory. Do NOT modify verified_on array.
ApplyChanges.step5: PARAMETER COMPATIBILITY CHECK (framework rationality): Before saving, verify parameter changes preserve backward compatibility: (a) For each modified parameter in params section, compare against previous version: TYPE_CHANGE (string→object, number→string, etc.) → YELLOW 'PARAMETER_TYPE_CHANGE: {param} type changed {old_type}→{new_type}. Dependent modules or external callers may break.' REQUIRED_CHANGE (optional→required) → RED 'PARAMETER_REQUIRED_CHANGE: {param} was optional, now required. This is a BREAKING CHANGE — existing callers passing null will fail. Recommend LEVEL_A version bump.' REMOVED_PARAM (param existed, now absent) → YELLOW 'PARAMETER_REMOVED: {param} removed. Verify no callers depend on it.' (b) Output compatibility_report = { type_changes: [{ param, old, new }], required_changes: [{ param }], removed: [{ param }], backward_compatible: boolean }. (c) If backward_compatible == false and version change is patch → WARNING 'Breaking parameter change detected but version bump is patch. Consider minor or major bump per SemVer.' Save atomic (.tmp then rename). Backup as .bak.
ApplyChanges.step7: RECONCILIATION OUTPUT (CR7): After all fixes are applied, produce modifystep_reconciliation = { fixes_applied: [{ finding_id: string, original_severity: string, fix_description: string, affected_file: string, affected_step: string }], total_fixes: number }. This output is consumed by main.aisop.json ModifyStep for RESEARCH2 RECONCILIATION — it marks corresponding Research2 findings as 'fixed_in_modifystep' so that Generate2 ThreeDimTest can perform targeted verification rather than independent re-discovery.
ApplyChanges.constraints: WRITE SAFETY: .bak backup. ATOMIC: .tmp then rename. Partial failure -> rollback from .bak.
ApplyChanges.Error: Write fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: display patched JSON as code block -> inform IMPORTANT. Error types: timeout(operation_timeout)->retry with backoff. parse_error(data_malformed)->retry(1) then fallback. io_error(file_write_failure)->retry(1) then fallback.

on_error.fallback: display patched JSON as code block
